meaning: n.救援,援助者,救援人员vt.援助,救援
examples: They are HIS billows, whether for our succor.
So sometimes the traveler is asked whence will come their succor.
Alas! Will no one come to the succor of the human soul in that darkness?
Whole Foods founder John Mackey built a retail powerhouse peddling food as sensual, succulent succor.
Obama has failed to succor the 20% of home owners still carrying negative equity on their homes.
